facts.text: Europe is a continent known for its dense concentration of nations, languages, and historical landmarks.
The Alps stretch across several European countries and include some of the continent's most famous mountain scenery.
The Danube River flows through more countries than any other river in Europe.
The European Union is a political and economic partnership that includes many, but not all, European countries.
Europe contains some of the world's most visited cities, including Paris, London, Rome, Barcelona, and Amsterdam.
The continent was the birthplace of the Renaissance, the Enlightenment, and much of modern Western philosophy and science.
Russia spans both Europe and Asia, making it a transcontinental country.
The smallest country in the world, Vatican City, is located within Rome, Italy.
Europe's cultural diversity shows up in its food, architecture, languages, music, and regional traditions.
The euro is used by many European countries, though several nations keep their own currencies.
Europe's geography includes fjords in Norway, Mediterranean coasts, central plains, and high mountain ranges.
The continent played an enormous role in global exploration, empire building, trade, and industrialization.
European soccer leagues and tournaments are among the most watched sporting competitions in the world.
Historic sites like the Colosseum, Acropolis, Eiffel Tower, and Stonehenge make Europe a major destination for travelers and history fans.
The Eiffel Tower in Paris remains one of the most recognized landmarks in Europe.
Built for the 1889 Exposition Universelle, it became a global symbol of France and European travel.
Eiffel Tower is one of the most recognizable places or topics associated with Europe.
Travel guides about Europe frequently feature Eiffel Tower.
Learning about Eiffel Tower helps explain part of the geography, culture, or history of Europe.
For many visitors, Eiffel Tower stands out as a major highlight of Europe.
